safetyTips: Remember to never share your personal details — like your address or school — with anyone you don't know online. If someone you've never met is asking for your information, trust your gut and report it to a trusted adult straight away.
Heading out to search for this week's treasure? Don't go alone! Always let a parent or guardian know where you're going, and take a friend with you. It's more fun with someone else anyway.

If something or someone makes you feel uncomfortable while you're out on the hunt, don't ignore that feeling. Move to a public area, contact a trusted adult, and if you ever feel like you're in danger, call 10111 immediately.

Before you head out to search for the hidden item, make sure a parent or guardian knows exactly where you're going and when you plan to be back. A quick message or phone call goes a long way in keeping everyone in the loop and keeping you safe.

announcements.heading: Suspicious Vehicle in the Area
announcements.body: Before you head out to search for the hidden item, make sure a parent or guardian knows exactly where you're going and when you plan to be back. A quick message or phone call goes a long way in keeping everyone in the loop and keeping you safe.

announcements.heading: Stranger Approaching Teens at the Park
announcements.body: There have been a few reports of an unknown individual approaching groups of teens at Riverside Park, asking personal questions and offering lifts. Please avoid engaging with this person, do not accept anything from them, and report any suspicious behaviour to your parents or local authorities straight away.

announcements.heading: Fake Online Giveaway Scam
announcements.body: A fake social media account has been circulating in the area, claiming to offer cash prizes in exchange for personal details like ID numbers, addresses and school names. This is a scam. Do not engage with this account or share any information. Report the account and let a trusted adult know if you've already been in contact with it.
